content: i wouldn't trust that stream. flares admitted that it was mostly one viewer doing most of the heavy lifting instead of him

you can always replay chapters on story difficulty at a later point. That difficulty just has the cutscenes and removes the fights. And for any questions regarding the story: Niko is the lorekeeper for a good reason, ask in here whenever something is unclear <:Gladge:1319410892907085976>
also he said the story was mid because it wasn't as dark as some chinese novels lol
ehh thats a matter of opinion so id let that pass
theres other gachas where i dont care too much about the story either
my beef is that hes definitely not the most reliable guy for story since he finds it forgettable
like how you wouldn't ask someone to reccomend the best place for a food they dont like
I do agree with him on story having some issues
I genuinely am asleep at the wheel during chapter 1-4 and only really massively enjoyed chapter 5-6 in arc 1
But I know a friend that really enjoyed initial chapters
So I chalked it up to me thing
oh the story definitely has issues
but evaluating it based on darkness is silly
darkness is a metric for edgelords
I think the setting is p grim, it's just not in your face about it
well as light put it
the setting is "nihilistic" more than "dark"
True
I would say especially act 2 feels "hopeful dark" things are messed up but the keeper takes steps to make things better, no matter how tiny the odds of success
Sylvester just gave up trying to tell Miryam lol
that was the last time that the Keeper neglected their homework
Dedicated priestess right there
The cult is all that matters for Myriam lol
She needs to bring forth the Divine Realm, can't blame her /jk
OK so I'm now out of A2C2 and I'm just making sure I have the timeline of events right

Morimens comes out, it gets to Chapter 7 of Arc 1, the devs run into big financial problems and are worried their game will EOS

So they were going to do a GDoll and a Clem story, but they had to scrap both and rush C8 and the finale - C8 is just a bit rushed with some reveals that would have been slower paced, but C9 is sort of assembled out of panicked crunch?
So then the insane crunch pays off and they get bailed out
i think the financial problems were already hitting around ch7
which is why its such a weird chapter
they were trying to see if they could pick up the gooner market
thats just my speculation though
So they go back to what they wanted to do with GDoll and Clem and they rebuild Clem's chapter into A2C2?
But with GDoll they can't really fix what they had do do in C9
So the GDoll focus in A2C1 is more about making sure they can actually use that character
i think this is a reasonable guess
Likewise they made sure to add William into A2C2 and give his backstory a bit because that also got cut?
we will never know their actual behind the scenes logic i think
until 5 years later when light gives an interview reminiscing or something
Yeah it was probably quite rough
And traumatic
It's really not easy losing people from a game dev team

I'm going to put in the next feedback letter what I'd like I guess - it would be good to have, _somewhere_, an outline of the GDoll-shows-up-at-Mythag story that got cut, and some notes on what the original plans were.
Because C8 and especially C9 really leave a bad taste in the mouth played through now given, like, Morimens obviously hasn't EOS'd
pretty sure niko has an entire thread on this
I'd like it in app, along some kind of reading order
It's just a real shame that you go from some really peak chapters in Murphy's arc into C7, which I think is the best Morimens is in terms of story telling because it's so tightly written
and then it kinda, uh, melts
and then you get to A2C1 and A2C2 and it's peak again and I'm like "why did I have to suffer, BIAV?!"
I'm fairly sure the entire thing with Thais and Aeon of Desire was planned long before the release <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>
i think the thais PV is such a huge tonal shift
it has to be intentional
I really liked C7. The two-chapters-per-story format was slow.
C7 is just this tightly-written rollercoaster that is surprising all the way through (even if the villain is _hilariously_ obvious)

If C8 was GDoll showing up and the mystery about other universes, and then C9 was C8 but some of the lore dumps/plot progression moved to GDoll's chapter, it'd be ideal IMO.

It'd be really nice if the CN/SK release was able to budget some work there but I get that it's really hard to justify that sort of expense.
Anyway, I was bummed out, then I really liked how the story has recovered and it's given me a lot of confidence in the team.
i thought ch7 was weak because it was culturally insensitive
like if you know anything at all about the middle east then its so silly
I did think that too
my favorite chapters are the pure classic horror ones like ch2
I'm looking forward to doing A2C2 again when the translation pass is done
of all the chapters to still be stuck using GPT translation it had to be the psychological drama
I really enjoyed a2c4 now that I've finally finished it. Very interesting premise to go with || a time loop but not really, instead an artificial domain that is looping||
Chapter 7 is ancient egypt/mesopotania/numidia vibe, not really arabic, and it fits the vibe of the Lovecraft-era of pulp speculative horror/etc
so it gets a pass
but it is close
remember
Ryker is xenophobic
problematic furry
Yep
For chapter 7 i honestly didn't even think of doing any serious cultural associations, just felt pure lovecraftian to me
my problem with chapter 7 is it should have been 2 chapters like 5-6
they are literally wearing egyptian headdresses
A lot of that chapter is westerners intruding on a different culture and fucking everything up
the pacing is too tight and too many characters that were not utilized well
*ancient egyptian* headdresses
yeah
like 5000 years out of date
aramites larping
Because it's an ancient civilisation that's been locked in this insane fight against dissolution where there is no progress, only a cycle of rebirth/death whilst trying to outrun the apocalypse
The entire chapter is kinda about how they and their ways are stuck in the past
Alva even tried to change that
Thais is forced to set up the cycle and Alva's entire character is attempting to find some way out of it but failing
yeah but "haha look at this backwater tribe of brown people who need to be taught the correct way to live by white saviors" is NOT a good look
iirc they actually just call them savages multiple times
have you considered they just didn’t research anything and just went with the most common misconception of egyptian
maybe
also I love Ryker but he being way too xenophobic for my taste in that chapter
atleast Ryker in his special ops is better
at least xenophobia is period accurate
especially to the genre
It's all a good point tho
It's a tightrope of a chapter in a lot of ways and I like it so much because I think it pulls it off when it really shouldn't
it lucky it was released 6 months after launch
when most players are still CN
yeah
it's lucky that the game was only picked up globally by anyone once it was clear its theme was a one-off
I can imagine someone picking the game up when C7 was released would feel like they got baited
my favourite chapters will always be arc 1 ch5-6 and arc 2 2-3
I think they were the best overall in terms of atmosphere and enjoyment for me
C2 is unintentionally very funny repeatedly
It is supposed to be creepy but having a horde of Clementines just chasing the Keeper just is a very silly visual
The writer leans into it a few times tbf
i think they couldve went into more bug horror
the only touch upon it proper during the mind space
The entire moment where the Keeper opens a door and Albino is just like 'go away I'm on holiday' too
It has this slightly madhouse flair to it which is a change of pace
im a big fan of psychological horror
silent hill my beloved
Herbert and Albino both being there is kinda funny
because Albino is actually there on holiday so he is resting and doesnt want anything to do with the keeper
and Herbert just does Herbert thing
Little William being a menace who makes explosives with baking powder was also great

I like how the Keeper is like WILLIAM STOP RUNNING and they never stop to consider that William, who presumably has no Silver Key at all, literally has no other option other than to run away from everything
The man is having to speedrun the plot
Actually finding William in chapter 4 was wonderful though. Was worried entire act 2 would just be chasing ghosts xD

吴侬软语挠心脾，轻罗小扇掩笑吟。
郎情妾意曲终尽，屠戮礼祭唤神女。
朱门内眷无人忆，化作枯骨悲了去。

宅深巷远，连梦都飞不出去；凄凄哀嚎，在扇与镰中消弭。
“老爷…郎君…好痛、好痛……”
滋养吧——生长吧——从欲望里从痴妄中
the top part is a poem
im not that good with classical chinese so i couldnt tell you what kind of poem
but its written in meter and all that
磐龙
this is Panlong CN name I think?

I just unlocked Ramona: Timeworn and learn about the Genesis form of characters. I think we have four Genesis Awakeners now? (Ramona, Helot, Doll, and Murphy? Please cmiiw.) Can anyone tell me if we will also have a chapter or episode about those Genesis characters and how they get to Mythag?

We will also have Genesis Tulu releasing in arc 2. Ramona and Gramona can't exist in the same world due to spiritual interferences, so Gramona has to borrow Ramona's body in order to interact with The Keeper physically.
Hold on, does this mean the other Genesis Awakeners can exist in the same world?
And now that I know we'll get a proper Cthulhu, I think I will skip everyone until his banner comes lol
For that i have no idea if it has been explained. Ramona happens to possess a fragment of Tawil, making it stand out
Good luck with that, i'd admire your dedication if you succeed
Cthulhu fthagn
Do we get to meet the other Genesis Awakeners in the stories?
Preferably main story

Yup, definitely. We meet Gramona in the Psyche deepdive. Ghelot as well, Gdoll has been cut from arc 1 and remains unexplained to this day as to why she was in Mythag. We meet Gmurphy in arc 2 chapter 4
Why the devs did GDoll so dirty 😭
For Gdoll it was because the game was near EOS, causing everything to be rushed
~~I saw GDoll and I immediately thought I could fix her~~
We do meet her again in arc 2 chapter 1

I hope one day they will do it yeah

She's the writer trust

Could anybody explain what exactly the committee's role is??
Okay but imagine: Mark Twain (Yomangu’tho)
go into the archive and look at the organizations page
they are on there
None of the organization pages are very in depth

the MTC is the final say in policies surrounding awakeners. Their role in practice comes down to:
1. Ensuring the existence of awakeners doesn't become public information 
2. Policing awakeners and punishing awakeners that break the rules
3. Determining the rights of awakeners 
4. Responding to Dissolution related incidents (they sometimes collaborate with mythag for this)
